commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=454c0489d5865182e886003f24f8a642cb08ad2a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes model configuration planning to omit plaintext provider apiKey values from generated models.json while keeping marker-based auth references and adds regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main serializes finalProviders into models.json, and the PR removes plaintext apiKey values after merge. The merge blocker is also source-reproducible from pi-coding-agent 0.75.1 requiring apiKey for custom provider model entries.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the PR changes secret persistence, has a P1 upgrade/auth compatibility finding, and lacks real runtime proof.

target.title: fix: avoid serializing plaintext model api keys
target.body:
## Summary
- Prevent plaintext provider API keys from being serialized into generated models.json contents.
- Preserve non-secret auth markers such as env/secretref references so runtime auth still works.
- Add regression coverage for new plaintext config values, managed env secret markers, and existing models.json merges.

Fixes #11829.

## Verification
- npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.auth-provenance.test.ts src/agents/model-auth-markers.test.ts
- npm exec --yes pnpm@11.1.0 -- tsgo:core
- git diff --check

Result: targeted Vitest run passed 4 files / 40 tests, tsgo:core passed, and git diff --check passed.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

This reduces token persistence by stripping plaintext apiKey values before writing models.json, while preserving marker-based runtime auth references.

## Notes
I did not verify a full interactive OpenClaw agent runtime session with live provider credentials. The regression tests exercise the affected planner serialization boundary directly.
